QF.id: Registration, Code of Conduct and General Obligations_40

question.text: Under Regulation 21A, which type of director is EXEMPT from the "common director" definition of associate, provided they recuse themselves from relevant decisions on both boards?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 21A, while a common director generally makes the MB an "associate" of the issuer, two exceptions exist: (i) nominee directors and (ii) independent directors who recuse themselves from relevant decisions on both boards. **The Trap:** Executive, managing, and whole-time directors are all deeply involved in operational decisions and cannot claim the exemption — they would create an associate relationship. **Cross-Exam Context:** Independent director exemptions appear in corporate governance topics tested in NISM Series IX and are conceptually linked to Companies Act provisions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Even with the exemption, the independent director must formally recuse from BOTH boards' decisions — document the recusal meticulously.
